audit_items.proof_trace: At L1557, `uprv_free(p)` is called where `p` is a `char**` traversal pointer initialized to `regions`; on the first iteration this frees the `regions` array itself, and `uprv_free(regions)` at L1559 double-frees the same block. Subsequent iterations call `uprv_free` on interior (non-heap-start) addresses of the already-freed block, corrupting heap metadata. The destructor (~TZDBNames, L1466–1470) uses the correct `uprv_free(*p)` (dereference) pattern, confirming L1557 is a typo where `*p` was written as `p`.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker who can supply or trigger loading of a timezone resource bundle containing `parseRegions` entries where `ures_getStringByIndex` returns failure (malformed resource data) sets `regionError=TRUE`, triggering the double-free path via any ICU timezone/date formatting API (e.g., `TZDBTimeZoneNames::getMetaZoneNames` → `TZDBNames::createInstance`), leading to heap corruption and potential code execution.
